summary: The Dockerized reproduction starts Gogs v0.13.4, commits a symlinked repository path as a low-privilege repo writer, uploads a crafted filename through the web upload flow, and proves that Gogs writes `/tmp/evohunt_owned.txt` outside the repository root while the bare repo tree remains unchanged.
expected_security_impact: The run should create `/tmp/evohunt_owned.txt` with attacker-controlled content from the upload flow even though the bare repository tree still contains only the committed `pivot` symlink, proving the write escaped the repository root.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned upstream Gogs `v0.13.4` at `d958a47a0e9d8747e399c687fdb3ec64a3b1a736`, built the reproduction image, started the target service, and printed `PROOF: wrote /tmp/evohunt_owned.txt outside the repository root while the repo tree remained ['pivot']`.
verification_method: The helper verifies two conditions: `/tmp/evohunt_owned.txt` exists with the expected attacker-controlled content, and `git --git-dir <repo-root>/attacker/ownedrepo.git ls-tree -r --name-only master` still returns only `pivot`. Together these checks distinguish an escaped filesystem write from a normal in-repository upload or an unrelated startup artifact.
evidence_produced_by_run: Container output included the exact proof string `PROOF: wrote /tmp/evohunt_owned.txt outside the repository root while the repo tree remained ['pivot']`.
The helper confirmed the proof file content matched `owned by evohunt\n`.
The helper confirmed the bare repository tree still contained only `pivot` after the upload flow completed.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the public Gogs repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable release commit, copies that checkout into the Docker build context, and also mounts the checked-out repository read-only into the container at /target-repo.
